page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific single-threaded operations. The server achieves top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.43 GB/sec and strong performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.68 GB/sec. Multi-core stress-ng performance registers at 20536.75, while the PassMark CPU score is 33289.29. Memory bandwidth tests show 1518.31 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 139.09 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In language model tasks, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 364.6 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, alongside average performance on larger 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20s_v7 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local disks and GPUs reduces overhead for architectures relying on external Premium Storage. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM-to-vCPU ratios without the added cost of dedicated accelerator hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It delivers 20 dedicated vCPUs (10 physical cores) and 160.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 35.43 GB/sec and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. The server is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations and caching layers, where high memory density is required without local storage overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: An Intel-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 160 GB of RAM and 20 dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
160.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation
Premium Storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
